stem: A software testing study surveys 20 test cases and observes 11 positive outcomes out of 20 (55.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (13 observations, rate = 46.2%) and Group B (7 observations, rate = 71.4%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 45% and 59% of the population. Estimate the true software bug rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.55. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.5789 (range [0.5607, 0.5972]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.3421, 0.7418]. Point estimate: 0.5789.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.52: 0.52×0.454 + 0.48×0.7143 = 0.5789.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
